Recently, the city Council approved the creation of a connunity

District (west camarillo community Facilities Dis-
trict No. L) and authorized the sale of bonds therefor. At the
hearing on the creation of the comnunity Facilities District, r

enployed as City Attorney also being enployed under separate

were authorized. My concern centered upon the ability of the
City Attorney to provide objective advice on the boundaries of
the District, the legality of the proceedings undertaken, and
the facitities 
for which the bond proceeds \,rere goinq to be
used when the Attorney had a direct and substantial financial
interest in the positive outcome of the proceedings.
As might be expected, the Attorney advised the City council

\.rou1d have been ernployed by contract with the City. I argued

as the City Attorney did. Notwithstanding my conurents, the

sections.question_synthetic: Does a conflict of interest exist when a law firm serving as City Attorney also serves as Bond Counsel for a community facilities district, given the firm's financial interest in the district's approval?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. Document is a complaint/inquiry from a private citizen requesting FPPC investigation. Some OCR errors present in original text (e.g., 'centlenen', 'facitities', 'cj-rcumstances') but meaning is clear.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Does a conflict of interest exist when a law firm serving as City Attorney also serves as Bond Counsel for a community facilities district, given the firm's financial interest in the district's approval?

embedding.summary: Arthur Goulet, a Camarillo resident and property owner, requests that the FPPC investigate whether a conflict of interest exists when the city's contracted law firm (Burke, Williams and Sorensen) serves simultaneously as City Attorney and Bond Counsel for a newly created Community Facilities District.
